title: vandenboschia dilatata
intro: Vandenboschia dilatata, also known as the forked spleenwort or Australian forked spleenwort, is a species of fern native to Australia and New Zealand. It is a type of epiphytic fern that grows on tree trunks and branches. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Vandenboschia dilatata include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for consumption.
